content: Based on these information, what are the assets that could be used to make some quick profit

args.thought: User wants actionable asset ideas for quick profit (~3 weeks) based on our established bearish Nasdaq thesis. Need to identify: (1) Best inverse ETFs for shorting NDX/QQQ, (2) Any long opportunities in defensive sectors, (3) Clear risk/reward for each. User can only buy assets directly (no options). Key constraints: 3-week hold means avoiding high decay ETFs like QID.

args.thought: Analyzing the inverse ETF data retrieved. For a 3-week hold on bearish Nasdaq thesis: PSQ (-1x) at $31.66, QID (-2x) at ~$22-23, SQQQ (-3x) at ~$87. Key concern: decay. SQQQ has highest decay risk for 3-week hold due to daily reset compounding. QID is moderate decay. PSQ minimal decay but lower leverage. Need to verify YTD performance on defensive assets (XLP, XLU, XLV, GLD, GDX) since get_stock_prices failed - will use web_search for YTD data.

args.thought: Final synthesis: For 3-week bearish Nasdaq play, PSQ is safest (minimal decay, -1x), QID offers balanced risk/reward (-2x, moderate decay), SQQQ highest risk/reward (-3x, significant decay). Defensive longs: GLD YTD ~18-20% (strong momentum), GDX YTD ~18% but volatile, XLU/XLP likely flat to slightly positive (defensive but low beta). Best quick profit assets ranked: (1) QID for direct Nasdaq short with manageable decay, (2) GLD for defensive long with momentum, (3) PSQ for conservative inverse exposure.
